write_path.not_comparable_to_openpuffer: openpuffer MinIO harness: WAL-limited upsert+index wait (~1 durable commit/s, docs/s ingest tiers in op-scaling-*.json). turbopuffer publishes per-write commit latency (≤200ms) and namespace write throughput (thousands/s)—different durability/throughput model; do not compare op ingest_docs_per_sec to tpuf writes_per_sec_per_namespace_claim.

verdict_text: Critical: openpuffer measured 100.0k×128 cold p50 ~880 ms is the same order of magnitude as turbopuffer official 874 ms @ 10M×1024 (~1.01×)—**not comparable** (100× fewer docs, 8× fewer dims, MinIO vs GCP fleet, different load); if doc-count scaling on this harness held to 10M, canonical linear extrapolation gives ~87321 ms @ 10M×128 (~99.9× tpuf cold 874 ms—low confidence, unmeasured); tiers (10.0k=96ms / 50.0k=412ms / 100.0k=880ms) imply power-law β≈0.95, √dim heuristic ~246981 ms (~283× cold). Warm path (POST /warm + eventual, MinIO disk cache): 10.0k warm=112ms (~8.0× tpuf 14ms @ 10M), 100.0k warm=827ms (~59.1× tpuf 14ms @ 10M)—**not comparable** to tpuf fleet NVMe @ 10M×1024; openpuffer MinIO ingest+index throughput is 10.0k=909 docs/s (11s wall), 50.0k=3571 docs/s (14s wall), 100.0k=758 docs/s (132s wall) (WAL-limited upsert+index wait) vs turbopuffer's published ≤~200 ms durable write-commit latency and up to ~10,000 writes/s per namespace (tradeoffs doc) (object-storage WAL—not comparable to openpuffer WAL ingest docs/s); extrapolated openpuffer is **~283× slower** than tpuf 874 ms — **not** in the same absolute ballpark on this MinIO harness. Treat as scaling-shape signal only—do not read 100k≈874 ms as parity; 10M openpuffer is unmeasured.
